title: Why Do We Tell Patients They Can't Have Procedures When Pregnant Or Breastfeeding？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Why Do We Tell Patients They Can_t Have Procedures When Pregnant Or Breastfeeding？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: Why do we tell patients that they can't have procedures when they're pregnant or breastfeeding [Music] really common question particularly breastfeeding people think that can't be a connection you know particularly with dermal filler where we know it's staying in the skin how is …

segments.text: why do we tell patients that they can't have procedures when they're pregnant or breastfeeding [Music] really common question particularly breastfeeding people think that can't be a connection you know particularly with dermal filler where we know it's staying in the skin how is this possibly going to interact with breast milk and the reason is it's basically one of the fundamental kind of rules of medical care is that you don't increase the risk in any way for babies and particularly if they're in utero and if you can just wait and do the procedure later unless you've got some very good reason so it should be in the patient's best interest some really significant reason why the parent needs it that's that and then a very low risk of any complication now we don't have any data on these things and this is one of the things that means this is such a common rule is no one is ever going to run a trial where we inject pregnant women with botulinum toxins or dermal filler and see what happens in 30 years time to their kids is simply never going to happen so we

don't know there's a degree of uncertainty we need to respect that what we're talking about is the future of an individual who might live for 100 years with the problem that you may or may not cause them and certainly what we don't want is any uncertainty for the patient as well so i haven't think one of the best reasons not to do it is if you think about you know even stillbirth is one in 200 if the patient just makes a connection that they had botox three weeks before their um baby was stillborn that can cause them a huge amount of psychological stress which i don't have anything to do with so there are little things like that similar with cut death you know imagine they're breastfeeding and you have a you do some toxin on them and then the baby dies and that's really going to cause a lot of problems for them and for you they might have psychological stress over the decision they made and wonder for the rest of their lives whether there was some cause now you may think there's no cause at all i mean i personally don't think there will be any connection with those two things but you don't want that question mark so um it's a really good idea

not to increase the uncertainty during what is essentially quite an uncertain time pregnancy uh breastfeeding to less of an extent but still something worth thinking about cause you a lot of headaches and just cause unnecessary stress why not wait until it's a simple procedure with no complicating factors so i always say why not wait until you've got calm seas before you go out sailing there's no point setting out in a storm and that's a bit what it's like doing if you do a procedure on someone who's pregnant and breastfeeding there's uncertainty that you can easily just wait a bit longer for and get away from and have a much easier life so that's why we don't treat people who are pregnant and breastfeeding
